message:
fix(runtime): harden ID allocation against overflow and fix generation wraparound comparison

Three runtime correctness improvements:

1. Checked ID increments (kernel.rs, state.rs):

   ControllerRegistry::next_ledger_id and RuntimeState::next_finalizer_id
   now use checked_add(1).expect("...overflow") instead of bare += 1.
   While overflow is astronomically unlikely in practice, a silent
   wraparound to 0 would violate the monotonicity invariant that
   downstream code depends on for deduplication and ordering. An
   explicit panic on overflow makes the invariant self-documenting.

2. Wraparound-safe generation comparison (intrusive_heap.rs):

   The FIFO tiebreaker in IntrusivePriorityHeap previously used a bare
   gen_a < gen_b comparison, which breaks when the u64 generation
   counter wraps around. Replaced with gen_b.wrapping_sub(gen_a)
   .cast_signed() > 0, which correctly handles wraparound: if gen_a
   was assigned before gen_b (within half the u64 range), the signed
   difference is positive, preserving FIFO order across the wrap point.

3. Sleep repoll test cleanup (sleep.rs):

   The future_repoll_after_completion_panics test previously used
   catch_unwind to verify the panic, then asserted the result. Replaced
   with the standard #[should_panic(expected = "...")] attribute, which
   is both simpler and gives better diagnostics on failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
